title: Protests over NEET paper leak intensify nationwide
author: The Hindu Bureau
content_html:
What began as a protest at Jantar Mantar against the National Eligibility cum Entrance Test (NEET) paper leak over a month ago snowballed into a nationwide agitation on Thursday (July 23, 2026), with student outfits, civil society groups and political parties hitting the streets and demanding the resignation of Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan, while criticising the police crackdown on protesters in Delhi.

In Chennai, the protests sought the scrapping of NEET. Amid the ongoing agitation by the Cockroach Janta Party-Tamil Nadu and the All India Student Federation (AISF) at the Communist Party of India office, the Neelam Student Movement also organised a demonstration. The Democratic Youth Federation of India, the Student Federation of India (SFI), and over 500 students of New College at Royapettah in the heart of the city joined the cause.

Across Kerala, the SFI took out protest marches that turned confrontational in several districts as police used water cannon and tear gas to disperse the crowds. Marches were held in Kochi, Pathanamthitta, Alappuzha, Thrissur, Palakkad and Kannur, while protesters clashed with police in Kasaragod and faced water cannon in Palakkad and Kannur.

‘Chalo Lok Bhavan’

The “Chalo Lok Bhavan” protest organised by student/youth organisations in Andhra Pradesh turned tense after the police stopped them midway. They said Friday’s proposed educational institutions’ bandh would be intensified and the agitation would continue in phases until the Education Minister resigned.

In Uttar Pradesh’s Lucknow, Bhim Army activists tried to march towards the Governor’s residence, clashed with the police when stopped, and were detained. The Sanyukt Chhatra Manch organised a march in Prayagraj, leading to a brief face-off with the police. Protests were also held in Ghazipur, Varanasi, Ballia, and Azamgarh.

The Aam Aadmi Party’s Chandigarh unit extended support to activist Sonam Wangchuk and demanded Mr. Pradhan’s resignation. The party workers and social activists in Mandi, Himachal Pradesh, criticised the police action in Delhi.

Besides, the student agitation in Madhya Pradesh’s Indore gathered momentum as more students, teachers and parents joined in solidarity with the Delhi protests, leading to a heavy police deployment. Student groups expressed their grievances in Bhopal. Hundreds of working professionals, organisations such as the Dalit Sangharsha Samithi and the All India Students’ Association (AISA), gathered in Karnataka’s Bengaluru to voice their anguish.

The Delhi protests also resonated in the northeastern States. In Assam, students’ organisations, including the All Assam Students’ Union, the All Assam Minority Students’ Union, and the All Assam Tribal Students’ Union came together. Students, activists, and political leaders in Guwahati sought education reforms, where the protest was organised under the Cockroach Janata Party (CJP). Rallies were taken out in Arunachal Pradesh’s Itanagar, Shillong in Meghalaya, and Mizoram’s Aizawl. Meanwhile, the BJP Assam unit organised a counter-protest in Guwahati against the Congress’s sit-in outside the Prime Minister’s residence in Delhi.

Several cases

In Bihar, the police lodged several cases against over 600 unidentified protesters for vandalising public properties and Wednesday’s protest march in which several police personnel were injured. Over 40 students were detained in Patna.

Security personnel resorted to baton charge against stone-pelters in Katihar and Begusarai. Protests were also organised in Bhagalpur and Darbhanga districts, and marches taken out in Patna, Munger, Sheikhpura and Vaishali. In Patna, members of AISA and the All India Progressive Women’s Association (AIPWA) extended support to those gathered at Jantar Mantar.

The Jan Sangharsh Manch Haryana and the Students’ Organisation for Socialist Democracy activists took to the streets in Haryana’s Kurukshetra. Leaders of all major Khap Panchayats in the district came together and in Jind, the district bar association suspended work for a day in support.

Chhattisgarh’s capital Raipur saw the police booking nearly a dozen Congress workers under non-bailable provisions.

The Biju Janata Dal’s (BJD) youth wing staged a demonstration in Odisha, where all the Opposition parties have intensified their agitation along with students’ outfits. In Maharashtra, the Brihanmumbai Municipal Corporation (BMC) House witnessed chaotic scenes as Opposition parties demanded a discussion on the police action against agitators in Delhi. Various students’ wings, such as AISA and SFI, organised rallies in several localities of Kolkata.

Published - July 24, 2026 12:51 am IST

  • Copy link
  • Email
  • Facebook

  • LinkedIn
  • WhatsApp
  • Reddit

title: He broke the fast in the presence of two Union Ministers on certain conditions, he says; Govt gave a written assurance that cases against students will be taken back, if they are not violent, Mr. Dorjay says
author: The Hindu Bureau
content_html:
Sonam Wangchuk broke his fast on Friday (July 24, 2026), said Cherring Dorjay Lakruk, co-convenor of the Leh Apex Body. He broke the fast in the presence of two Union Ministers, Mr. Dorjay said.

He broke the fast on certain conditions. The government gave a written assurance that cases against students would be taken back if they were not violent, said Mr. Dorjay. The government also gave a written assurance that they will consider compensation for suicide victims, he said.

Activist Sonam Wangchuk on Thursday urged supporters of the ongoing students’ movement to remain peaceful, saying he was pained by reports that “anti-social elements” were trying to provoke violence elsewhere while the protest at Jantar Mantar remained “peaceful”.

Marking the 26th day of his indefinite hunger strike, Wangchuk said in a post on X, “Peace and only peace is my way.” “While at Jantar Mantar the protests remain peaceful, I’m pained to learn that elsewhere some antisocial elements are taking advantage of the peaceful protests to provoke violence,” he said.

“No matter what the other side does our response must only be flowers. Please keep this tradition at all costs,” Wangchuk added.

His appeal came days after the July 20 ‘Sansad Chalo’ march, during which the Delhi Police stopped thousands of protesters from marching towards Parliament by using tear gas and baton charges. Protesters have also alleged the use of pellet guns and tasers.

In the aftermath of the crackdown, the Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) alleged that attempts were being made to portray the student movement as violent and to link it with incidents elsewhere in the national capital. The allegations were rejected by the police.

Earlier in the day, Wangchuk’s wife Gitanjali Angmo also defended the protesters, saying it was “deeply painful” to see students being portrayed negatively.

In another post on X later in the day, Angmo praised the conduct of the young protesters.

“Watching India’s Gen Z over the past few days has filled me with hope,” she said, adding that they had “looked after strangers like family, shared food and water, cleaned and swept their own protest sites, cared for the elderly, sung Jana Gana Mana and songs of hope together, stayed disciplined under immense provocation” and kept the movement alive through “countless acts of selfless service”.

“If this is the generation we have raised, India’s future is in safe hands. Indian Gen Z... you are not just the future of this country. You are role models to the world,” she said, while thanking parents for raising “compassionate, responsible and courageous citizens”.

The CJP-led agitation began on June 20, demanding accountability over alleged irregularities in competitive examinations, reforms in the education system and the resignation of Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan.

Wangchuk, who joined the agitation on June 28 and has been on an indefinite hunger strike since then, is currently admitted to Medanta Hospital in Gurugram. He has offered to end his fast if the Centre assures that no punitive action will be taken against peaceful protesters who participated in the movement.

Published - July 24, 2026 01:14 am IST

  • Email

title: PM Modi promises 'more strict actions against paper leaks' amid CJP protests
author: ANI
content_html:
Prime Minister Narendra Modi on Thursday (July 23, 2026) promised that “more strict” action would be taken against paper leaks as the CJP-led protests continue over the alleged irregularities in the NEET-UG 2026 examination.

In a self-recorded video shared on X, the Prime Minister announced that the Union Cabinet will take up a draft Bill providing for Fast-Track Courts and stringent punishment for offenders. he said, “I have issued directions to departments to set up Fast-Track Courts”

The Opposition has been demanding the resignation of Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan and a detailed discussion on the NEET paper leak issue in the ongoing Monsoon Session of Parliament, with the controversy triggering protests inside and outside the House.

Along with the video, the Prime Minister wrote, “More strict actions against paper leaks to come in tomorrow’s Cabinet!”

In the video, Mr. Modi said, “Friends, I know that paper leaks are not an ordinary matter. For lakhs of students and their parents, it is extremely painful. And that is why, in the last two and a half months since the paper leak incidents, several major steps have been taken. The culprits have been caught; they are currently in jail,” he said.

PM said, “Our most important responsibility was to ensure that a whole year for the students is not wasted. It was very important to conduct the exams immediately. The government used its full strength to arrange exams for nearly 22 lakh students in the shortest possible time. And just five or six days ago, on the 19th, the results were also announced. News of the happiness of successful students is coming from all over the country.”

Stressing that the government was not stopping there, PM Modi said, “However, we are not among those who are satisfied with just that. And therefore, today I have issued directions to the departments to set up Fast-Track Courts. The departments worked tirelessly and, late last night, provided me with a draft. This draft, which includes provisions for Fast-Track Courts and stringent punishments, will be discussed in the Cabinet tomorrow,” he said.

The PM further said, “After incorporating suggestions from Cabinet colleagues, it will be given a final shape. Efforts will be made to get this Bill passed in the House as soon as possible, as the second week of the Parliament session begins on Monday.”

Mr. Modi said, “Nothing is more important than the welfare and future of our youth! Those who try to harm the future of our youth will not be spared.”

Earlier in the day, Prime Minister Narendra Modi broke his silence on the controversy, announcing that the government would establish fast-track courts to ensure swift punishment for those involved in examination paper leaks.

Published - July 24, 2026 01:47 am IST

title: Delhi High Court notifies designated court to deal with criminal cases arising from exam paper leaks
author: PTI
content_html:
The Delhi High Court on Thursday (July 23, 2026) notified a designated court to deal with criminal cases arising from paper leaks as well as the use of other unfair means in public examinations.

The move comes after Prime Minister Narendra Modi's announcement on Thursday (July 23) that fast-track courts would be set up to swiftly deal with cases of paper leaks.

Also Read: Jantar Mantar protest updates on July 23

In a notification, the High Court administration said judicial officer Anu Grover Baliga's court shall function as a "Specially Designated Fast Track Court" to try offences under the Public Examinations (Prevention of Unfair Means) Act, 2024 and connected offences, from immediate effect.

The special court will function from the Rouse Avenue Court Complex.

Earlier in the day, amid continuing protests by students over the NEET paper leak, Prime Minister Modi said the welfare and interest of the country's youth are the government's top priority, and anyone trying to harm their future will not be spared.

PM Modi said the government decided to set up fast-track courts to ensure swift and stringent punishment for those involved in paper leaks.

He said he has directed the concerned authorities and officials to take all necessary steps on the issue of paper leaks.

Published - July 23, 2026 11:38 pm IST

title: No evidence against Sanjeev Mukhiya in NEET-UG 2024 case: CBI
author: The Hindu Bureau
content_html:
The Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI) on Thursday (July 23, 2026) said it did not find any evidence establishing the complicity of Sanjeev Kumar alias Sanjeev Mukhiya in the NEET-UG 2024 case. He is an accused in certain other cases being investigated by the Bihar police.

In 2024, the State police had detected the paper theft and registered a case. Prima facie suspicion had emerged at that stage against Sanjeev Mukhiya, suggesting his role, as he was found involved in certain other examination paper theft/leak cases, said the agency.

“He was accordingly named as an accused in the said FIR by the Bihar police. The case was subsequently transferred to the CBI for investigation,” it said.

The CBI said that during its investigation, each person involved in the theft and onward distribution of the stolen NEET-UG 2024 question paper were identified, as also each candidate who benefited from its use. “Multiple chargesheets have accordingly been filed by the CBI against 45 persons before the jurisdictional court in Patna,” said the agency.

“Sanjeev Mukhiya had remained absconding during the relevant time. He was subsequently arrested by the Bihar police in connection with those other cases. Since he was a named accused in the FIR in the NEET-UG 2024 case as well, the CBI took his police custody for the purpose of examination...,” it said.

As no evidence of his involvement in the NEET-UG case emerged, the CBI did not file any chargesheet against him. He accordingly availed bail in that case. “Sanjeev Mukhiya remained in judicial custody after his bail in the CBI’s case, he being an accused in certain other cases being investigated by the Bihar police,” said the CBI.

“This issue was clarified in the year 2024 itself adequately. The narrative going around now about his deliberate omission is factually incorrect,” it added.

Published - July 24, 2026 12:10 am IST

title: Three days since July 20 violence, Delhi Police, MHA yet to acknowledge police excesses
author: Vijaita Singh
content_html:
Three days after several videos of police using disproportionate force, including pellet guns, against unarmed men and women during the July 20 march to Parliament emerged on social media, the Delhi Police and the Ministry of Home Affairs (MHA) had yet to issue a statement or clarify their position.

Though Prime Minister Narendra Modi said on Thursday (July 23, 2026) that “a series of steps for safeguarding the interests of students” were being taken, there was no assurance from the government on probing the “police excesses” against the protesters.

On Wednesday (July 22), the Delhi High Court sought responses from the Centre and the Delhi Police on public interest litigation petitions alleging the excessive use of force against demonstrators during the July 20 march to Parliament called by the Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) to demand the resignation of Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan. Another case seeking an investigation into the CJP protest is listed for hearing on Friday (July 24).

The court also instructed the authorities to preserve all material connected to the police action, including CCTV footage and any video recordings of the incident. Over 100 police and Rapid Action Force (RAF) personnel were also injured in the incident.

The Hindu reported about two persons, who were hit by pellets allegedly fired by RAF personnel, being operated upon at government hospitals. One of them, Sahil Lochab (19), risks losing vision in the right eye due to a pellet piercing his pupil, his family said.

On Wednesday (July 22), Additional Deputy Commissioner of Police (ADCP) Sandeep Lamba, who was seen in a video slapping a woman on July 20, was removed from protest-related duties around Jantar Mantar and sent to north-east Delhi, his original place of posting.

Another video showed a police officer allegedly molesting a woman with a wooden stick.

Huge push

Recounting the July 20 incident, Eenesh (26), a resident of Chhattisgarh, who was among the first ones to be injured in police action near Jantar Mantar, said, “none of the protesters attacked the police. There were thousands of people in that small protest area which was blocked by high barricades. I was in the front when a huge push came from behind. Several people fell on the ground toppling the barricades. That is when the police started hitting us with batons. I saw 200 people being injured by police lathis.” Mr. Eenesh has been in Delhi since June 20 when the CJP protest started. He was on a hunger strike for seven days and continues to stay at the protest site with a bandaged wrist and knee. He refuted the police claim that protesters threw bricks at them. “You look around Jantar Mantar, there are no places where people could find loose rocks to throw at police. There was no space even to move,” Mr. Eenesh told The Hindu.

During the hearing before the Delhi High Court on Wednesday, Senior Advocate Gopal Sankaranarayanan, appearing for petitioner Umesh Kumar, said he had personally examined around 130 videos of the July 20 protest, which, according to him, showed widespread use of force by personnel purportedly belonging to the police.

He alleged that a large number of those deployed were either not in proper police uniform or, even if in uniform, were not wearing name badges, making it impossible to ascertain their identity. He further claimed that several personnel were carrying lathis and “shock-producing batons”, which, he argued, were not part of the standard equipment prescribed for the Delhi Police or the Rapid Action Force (RAF).

Referring to one of the videos placed before the court, Mr. Sankaranarayanan specifically named Additional Deputy Commissioner of Police Sandeep Lamba, alleging that the officer was seen slapping a woman who was standing on the roadside and was not participating in any act of violence.

“The videos show specifically, Mr. Lamba slapping a woman, who was standing innocently on the side and doing nothing. She is not moving, I show the video. Let us not be scared to name our thugs in uniform, when they deserve to be named. He should be summoned here (court) and asked to be accountable to his actions,” he said.

“There was no heat, no crowd or fury, completely unprovoked. He walks in past the woman, he reaches and slaps her across the face. These are the people we put in charge of our police force to protect us. That is why we are before this court,” Mr. Sankaranarayanan said.

The Senior Advovate contended that if constitutional courts failed to intervene against such instances of alleged police excess, it would effectively amount to granting law enforcement a licence to act with impunity.

“These kind of excessive force, if the constitutional courts do not step in and stem, it is a licence to the police that they will just get away with anything since they have the sanction of their political masters,” he said.

Senior Advocate N. Hariharan, also appearing for the petitioner, alleged that women protesters had been molested during the police action. “There are police officers who are molesting women using batons in their private parts if I may say so. It so ghastly,” he said. He submitted that police officers identifiable in the videos as having committed cognisable offences should face immediate registration of FIRs.

The senior advocates sought directions for the constitution of a Special Investigation Team (SIT), registration of FIRs against the policemen allegedly involved, departmental action against them, and a judicial inquiry into the incidents.

Published - July 24, 2026 02:14 am IST

  • Copy link
  • Email
  • Facebook
  • Twitter
  • Telegram
  • LinkedIn
  • WhatsApp
  • Reddit
SEE ALL Remove
content_text:
Three days after several videos of police using disproportionate force, including pellet guns, against unarmed men and women during the July 20 march to Parliament emerged on social media, the Delhi Police and the Ministry of Home Affairs (MHA) had yet to issue a statement or clarify their position.
Though Prime Minister Narendra Modi said on Thursday (July 23, 2026) that “a series of steps for safeguarding the interests of students” were being taken, there was no assurance from the government on probing the “police excesses” against the protesters.
On Wednesday (July 22), the Delhi High Court sought responses from the Centre and the Delhi Police on public interest litigation petitions alleging the excessive use of force against demonstrators during the July 20 march to Parliament called by the Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) to demand the resignation of Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan. Another case seeking an investigation into the CJP protest is listed for hearing on Friday (July 24).
The court also instructed the authorities to preserve all material connected to the police action, including CCTV footage and any video recordings of the incident. Over 100 police and Rapid Action Force (RAF) personnel were also injured in the incident.
The Hindu
reported about two persons, who were hit by pellets allegedly fired by RAF personnel, being operated upon at government hospitals. One of them, Sahil Lochab (19), risks losing vision in the right eye due to a pellet piercing his pupil, his family said.
On Wednesday (July 22), Additional Deputy Commissioner of Police (ADCP) Sandeep Lamba, who was seen in a video slapping a woman on July 20, was removed from protest-related duties around Jantar Mantar and sent to north-east Delhi, his original place of posting.
Another video showed a police officer allegedly molesting a woman with a wooden stick.
Huge push
Recounting the July 20 incident, Eenesh (26), a resident of Chhattisgarh, who was among the first ones to be injured in police action near Jantar Mantar, said, “none of the protesters attacked the police. There were thousands of people in that small protest area which was blocked by high barricades. I was in the front when a huge push came from behind. Several people fell on the ground toppling the barricades. That is when the police started hitting us with batons. I saw 200 people being injured by police lathis.” Mr. Eenesh has been in Delhi since June 20 when the CJP protest started. He was on a hunger strike for seven days  and continues to stay at the protest site with a bandaged wrist and knee. He refuted the police claim that protesters threw bricks at them. “You look around Jantar Mantar, there are no places where people could find loose rocks to throw at police. There was no space even to move,” Mr. Eenesh told
The Hindu
.
During the hearing before the Delhi High Court on Wednesday, Senior Advocate Gopal Sankaranarayanan, appearing for petitioner Umesh Kumar, said he had personally examined around 130 videos of the July 20 protest, which, according to him, showed widespread use of force by personnel purportedly belonging to the police.
He alleged that a large number of those deployed were either not in proper police uniform or, even if in uniform, were not wearing name badges, making it impossible to ascertain their identity. He further claimed that several personnel were carrying lathis and “shock-producing batons”, which, he argued, were not part of the standard equipment prescribed for the Delhi Police or the Rapid Action Force (RAF).
Referring to one of the videos placed before the court, Mr. Sankaranarayanan specifically named Additional Deputy Commissioner of Police Sandeep Lamba, alleging that the officer was seen slapping a woman who was standing on the roadside and was not participating in any act of violence.
“The videos show specifically, Mr. Lamba slapping a woman, who was standing innocently on the side and doing nothing. She is not moving, I show the video. Let us not be scared to name our thugs in uniform, when they deserve to be named. He should be summoned here (court) and asked to be accountable to his actions,” he said.
“There was no heat, no crowd or fury, completely unprovoked. He walks in past the woman, he reaches and slaps her across the face. These are the people we put in charge of our police force to protect us. That is why we are before this court,” Mr. Sankaranarayanan said.
The Senior Advovate contended that if constitutional courts failed to intervene against such instances of alleged police excess, it would effectively amount to granting law enforcement a licence to act with impunity.
“These kind of excessive force, if the constitutional courts do not step in and stem, it is a licence to the police that they will just get away with anything since they have the sanction of their political masters,” he said.
Senior Advocate N. Hariharan, also appearing for the petitioner, alleged that women protesters had been molested during the police action. “There are police officers who are molesting women using batons in their private parts if I may say so. It so ghastly,” he said. He submitted that police officers identifiable in the videos as having committed cognisable offences should face immediate registration of FIRs.
The senior advocates sought directions for the constitution of a Special Investigation Team (SIT), registration of FIRs against the policemen allegedly involved, departmental action against them, and a judicial inquiry into the incidents.

title: The National Democratic Alliance’s increased strength in the Lok Sabha since 2024 | Graphics
author: The Hindu Bureau
content_html:
Since June, there have been major rifts in the Trinamool Congress and the Shiv Sena (Uddhav Balasaheb Thackeray). Twenty MPs from the Trinamool rebelled to merge with the National Citizens Party of India, a Tripura-based, registered, and unrecognised political party. On June 19, 2026, six MPs from the Shiv Sena (UBT) defected to the Eknath Shinde camp of Shiv Sena. As a result, and amid the Opposition’s claims of these “illegal” and “unconstitutional” switches, the National Democratic Alliance’s strength in the Lok Sabha swelled by 26 seats.

The two graphics below show these 26 MPs, from the Trinamool and Shiv Sena (UBT), who joined the NCPI and Shiv Sena, respectively.

The NCPI aligns itself with the BJP-led NDA. With the addition of 26 MPs, the alliance’s strength in the Lok Sabha has increased to 318 from 293 seats.

The Trinamol and other Opposition parties have criticised the entry of the Trinamool rebels into the NCPI, with Congress general secretary (communication) Jairam Ramesh stating that the “bizarre move is part of the Union Home Minister’s strategy to engineer a two-thirds majority for the NDA in the Lok Sabha”. The defection of 20 rebels has reduced the Trinamool’s strength in the Lower House to eight and the NCPI has emerged as NDA’s strongest ally. Here is how the altered map of West Bengal’s Lok Sabha constituencies look like.

The monsoon session will conclude on August 17, 2026, and the speculation is rife that the government — with its newly-added strength — could revive the Constitution (131st Amendment) Bill, which sought to redistribute Lok Sabha seats based on the 2011 Census and implement women’s reservation. On April 17, 2026, the bill fell short of the required two-thirds majority and was defeated by a united Opposition. However, the Bill has not been included in the legislative agenda circulated among MPs.

The government has not formally confirmed or denied reports that it may introduce a revised version of the Bill during the session. The five bills listed by the government for the monsoon session include:

  • The Prevention of Insults to National Honour (Amendment) Bill, 2026, which seeks to amend the 1971 law to make the intentional insult of, or disruption during, the singing of the national song Vande Mataram a punishable offence.
  • Registration of Births and Deaths (Amendment) Bill, which proposes stricter provisions for delayed registration
  • The Income Tax (Amendment) Bill, 2026
  • The Supreme Court (Number of Judges) Amendment Bill, 2026
  • The Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises Development (Amendment) Bill, 2026

Published - July 23, 2026 11:01 pm IST

title: CPI criticises Kerala Chief Minister on Waqf issue
author: The Hindu Bureau
content_html:
The Communist Party of India (CPI) State secretariat took exception to the stand of Chief Minister V.D. Satheesan on the Waqf issue.

The reconstitution of the Board by including two non-Muslims had grown into a conflict issue between the Kerala Waqf Board and the State government. The Board had criticised the government for its reported support to petitions before the Kerala High Court seeking the reorganisation of the Board.

The Communist Party of India (Marxist) [CPI(M)] too had criticised the State government for its stand on the issue.

The CPI was of the view that the position adopted by Mr. Satheesan on the Waqf issue reflected the stance of the BJP. Though Mr. Satheesan wishes to describe himself as a Nehruvian activist, he was implementing the political policies of the killers of Gandhiji. He could also ensure the support of the Indian Union Muslim League, a constituent of the Congress-led United Democratic Front, in implementing these policies, the party said.

The party hoped that the followers of Gandhiji and Nehru within the Congress would shortly come out against the double standards of the Chief Minister.

Published - July 24, 2026 02:13 am IST

title: Governor emphasises need for coordination of tradition and technology
author: The Hindu Bureau
content_html:
Governor Thaawarchand Gehlot has emphasised the need for coordinating heritage with modern technology so as to introduce and popularise Ayurveda at the global level.

Inaugurating the convocation of Bhagavan Mahaveer Jain Ayurvedic Medical College in Gajendragad of Gadag district on Thursday, the Governor said that Ayurveda was not just a medical system to cure diseases but a scientific handbook for building a healthy lifestyle.

Mr. Gehlot said that Karnataka had a special place in Ayurveda and naturopathy and medical education institutions and universities in the state were building a better future for the youths.

Elaborating on the significance of Ayurveda, the Governor called on the budding Ayurveda practitioners to imbibe in their lives the ideals of Bhagavan Mahaveer and serve the society with commitment.

Terming Ayurveda as a result of generations of studies, research and experience, the Governor said that the basic objective of Ayurveda was to protect the health of a healthy man and to curing the patient’s diseases.

Referring to the ill effects of the stressful modern lifestyle, the Governor said that Ayurveda had an effective cure for the diseases borne out of modern lifestyle. Through Yoga and Ayurveda India had given an invaluable contribution to the world and every Indian should be proud of the ancient wisdom and heritage.

Chairman of the College Ashokkumar Bagmar presided over the convocation and spoke.  Secretary Ajitkumar Bagmar briefed about the institution and said that along with education all efforts were made to ensure that the students imbibed values. Principal Dr. Narayanrao made introductory remarks. Directors Sadeep Jinavasara, Tarabai Bagmar, Vardhaman Bagmar, Deputy Commissioner C.N. Sridhar, SP Rohan Jagadish and others were present.

Published - July 24, 2026 01:54 am IST
